sources.title: Arkansas politicians weigh-in on data center development, mindful of benefits and consequences
sources.excerpt: Terri Yarbrough Green in the 1st District ... emphasized the importance of public comment before new data center construction begins.

full_quote:
"There is a global arms race for data as we see greater investments in AI in nearly every sector of our economy. However, all this data isn't simply being used in an economic capacity," said Crawford, who has represented the state's 1st District since January 2011.

"What data is being collected, how much energy and water the center uses, how integrated it is with our energy grid, who is investing in it and how close it is to other critical infrastructure are all important national security questions," he said. "State and local governments must consider these factors carefully, and federal experts must continue studying them."

summary: House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence chair; joined bipartisan effort to stop AI chip smuggling to China; stated 'the global AI race against China is moving much faster than many think' and 'Export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment and AI chips have strengthened America’s advantage in the AI Arms Race'
full_quote:
House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) released the following statement after the Committee hosted an unclassified, full-Committee briefing with industry experts on the global artificial intelligence race against China.

“The global AI race against China is moving much faster than many think, and the stakes couldn’t be higher for U.S. national security. At this HPSCI briefing, Members heard directly from industry experts working on the cutting edge of AI development. The briefing helped inform our efforts to make sure the IC is incorporating advanced AI technology into its activities while staying ahead of our adversaries, most notably China. Frontier labs may soon achieve self-improving AI, and the U.S. must reach this threshold first to secure economic and national security dominance and potentially enjoy an enduring advantage over our adversaries.

sources.title: Crawford: The Global AI Race is Moving Much Faster Than Many Think
sources.excerpt:
House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) released the following statement after the Committee hosted an unclassified, full-Committee briefing with industry experts on the global artificial intelligence race against China.

“The global AI race against China is moving much faster than many think, and the stakes couldn’t be higher for U.S. national security. At this HPSCI briefing, Members heard directly from industry experts working on the cutting edge of AI development. The briefing helped inform our efforts to make sure the IC is incorporating advanced AI technology into its activities while staying ahead of our adversaries, most notably China. Frontier labs may soon achieve self-improving AI, and the U.S. must reach this threshold first to secure economic and national security dominance and potentially enjoy an enduring advantage over our adversaries.

sources.title: Chairman Moolenaar, Bipartisan Lawmakers Unveil Bill to Stop AI Chip Smuggling to China
sources.excerpt: House Select Committee on China Chairman John Moolenaar (R-MI) and Ranking Member Raja Krishnamoorthi (D-IL), along with Reps. Rick Crawford (R-AR), Bill Foster (D-IL), Josh Gottheimer (D-NJ), Bill Huizenga (R-MI), Darin LaHood (R-IL), and Ted Lieu (D-CA), today introduced the Chip Security Act, a bipartisan effort to stop advanced U.S. AI chips from falling into the hands of adversaries like the Chinese Communist Party (CCP).

sources.title: Chairmen Crawford & Mast Statement on Joint Briefing on Tech Competition with China
sources.excerpt: Export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment and AI chips have strengthened America’s advantage in the AI Arms Race. China is working to cheat and steal its way to winning through smuggling, distillation, and remote access of U.S. technology. America needs to secure our lead over the Chinese Communist Party by ending Beijing’s access to U.S. and allied technology and closing loopholes in our export controls.

"It's like a whole new world that's being built overnight, and it's not even overnight," he explained. "It's like every hour, something completely new is being created. The ability to think of something and then all of a sudden it becomes real is magical, and it's super exciting."

summary: Showed concern on how AI could rapidly replace white-collar jobs in an Arkansas Democrat Gazette article
full_quote:
Jones also expressed concern over how the embrace of AI could affect jobs. He recalled a conversation with technology leaders about how AI's growth will result in more work becoming automated.

"In the next three to five years, we could see 30% to 40% of the workforce eliminated. We have never seen that rate of change in the history of the world," Jones said. "Now, AI is replacing white-collar jobs at a rate we have never seen before, and it's doing it so fast that we don't have time to catch up."

summary: As a member of the AI Task Force, Hill endorsed its findings, which included that AI is critical to national security and that Congress should focus on overseeing AI activities for national security and expanding AI training at the Defense Department.

summary: Laid out two personal guiding principles: a "human in the loop" when using AI, and a requirement that firms disclose and document how they use it; sponsored the Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act to let companies experiment with AI through sandbox programs at federal financial agencies; favors light-touch, sector-specific approaches over broad mandates; said Congress doesn't want to put a rock on top of innovation
full_quote: "However, as with any innovation, risks give rise to new challenges. ... To move forward, we must embrace and adapt to innovation. … Identifying gaps and obstacles in our regulatory frameworks will help Congress create an AI landscape where innovation can flourish without unnecessary barriers, while ensuring robust consumer protections, and risk-based, technology-neutral regulation.”

sources.title: Financial Services Committee Examines Artificial Intelligence in Financial Services
sources.excerpt: "However, as with any innovation, risks give rise to new challenges. ... To move forward, we must embrace and adapt to innovation. … Identifying gaps and obstacles in our regulatory frameworks will help Congress create an AI landscape where innovation can flourish without unnecessary barriers, while ensuring robust consumer protections, and risk-based, technology-neutral regulation.”

sources.title: Chairman Hill Introduces Bipartisan Bill to Promote Artificial Intelligence in Financial Services
sources.excerpt: This bipartisan, bicameral legislation would promote Artificial Intelligence (AI) in financial services through regulatory sandboxes for AI test projects at federal financial regulatory agencies.

sources.title: RELEASE: REP. HILL APPLAUDS AI TASK FORCE’S REPORT ON GUIDING PRINCIPLES FOR ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
sources.excerpt: “In my view, two guiding principles should guide adoption. First, there should be a ‘human in the loop’ when using AI enhanced technology. Second, private sector firms should disclose and document how AI is being utilized when using AI in a customer facing or back-office efficiency activity. Company management should be able to ‘show their work’ and certify to their internal and external compliance officials that they are in full compliance with U.S. laws and regulations.

sources.title: Bipartisan group of lawmakers convinced AI legislation to break through Congressional deadlock
sources.excerpt: Congress doesn't "want to put a rock on top of innovation either and not allow it to flourish under the right circumstances," Rep. French Hill, an Arkansas Republican, said in August at a reception hosted by the Center for AI Safety. "It's a balancing act."

summary: Said proposed data centers offer extensive capital expenditure utility related funding for water, power generation, and grid expansion that exceed forecasted requirements, benefitting the surrounding communities by creating jobs and increasing the tax base
full_quote: "Here in Arkansas, some proposed data centers offer extensive capital expenditure utility-related funding for water, power generation, and grid expansion that far exceed any forecasted requirements of the proposed data center, which would benefit the surrounding communities while also creating jobs and increasing the tax base," the 2nd District congressman said in a statement.

summary: 2020 House Budget Committee hearing: stated 'Congress must ensure actions do not stifle innovation' and 'create regulatory environment that supports - not hinders - private industry' in AI development
full_quote:
While it will likely change the way many jobs are 
performed as technological advances have for many decades, we 
must harness the capabilities of AI to help drive our economy 
and society forward.Congress has to ensure that its actions do not stifle 
innovation, rather government should work in partnership with 
the private sector to move our country forward in AI research 
and development. By making strategic federal investments in AI R&D, 
Washington can help unleash America's pioneering and 
entrepreneurial spirit. It also means creating a regulatory 
environment that supports, not hinders, private industry by 
allowing technological advancements to flourish in a safe, 
trustworthy, and effective way.

sources.title:
MACHINES, ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE,
                   AND THE WORKFORCE: RECOVERING AND
                  READYING OUR ECONOMY FOR THE FUTURE
sources.excerpt:
While it will likely change the way many jobs are 
performed as technological advances have for many decades, we 
must harness the capabilities of AI to help drive our economy 
and society forward.Congress has to ensure that its actions do not stifle 
innovation, rather government should work in partnership with 
the private sector to move our country forward in AI research 
and development. By making strategic federal investments in AI R&D, 
Washington can help unleash America's pioneering and 
entrepreneurial spirit. It also means creating a regulatory 
environment that supports, not hinders, private industry by 
allowing technological advancements to flourish in a safe, 
trustworthy, and effective way.

summary: Libertarian candidate promised to 'champion technological innovation and progress' and 'advocate for policies that harness power of AI and emerging technologies' while ensuring 'privacy protections'
full_quote: As your representative, I promise to champion technological innovation and progress. I will advocate for policies that harness the power of AI and other emerging technologies to enhance government transparency, efficiency, and accessibility. By supporting secure, transparent voting systems using blockchain and promoting smart governance, we can streamline public services and boost public confidence. I am committed to ensuring that our technological advancements are balanced with robust privacy protections.

sources.title: Bobby Wilson (Arkansas) 2024 Survey
sources.excerpt: As your representative, I promise to champion technological innovation and progress. I will advocate for policies that harness the power of AI and other emerging technologies to enhance government transparency, efficiency, and accessibility. By supporting secure, transparent voting systems using blockchain and promoting smart governance, we can streamline public services and boost public confidence. I am committed to ensuring that our technological advancements are balanced with robust privacy protections.

summary: Describes the superintelligence race between US and China as a Cold War and deems it a national security issue
full_quote: We've also got national security issues that are around energy, and that gets back to artificial intelligence and data processing. There's in essence a new Cold War on who's going to get to super intelligence with AI first, and it's a race between us and China, predominantly.

sources.title: Unleashing America's Natural Resources with Bruce Westerman
sources.excerpt: We've also got national security issues that are around energy, and that gets back to artificial intelligence and data processing. There's in essence a new Cold War on who's going to get to super intelligence with AI first, and it's a race between us and China, predominantly.

summary: Pro-innovation because of the US-China race, but has said that "maybe humankind would've been better off if we didn't discover this"; supports permitting reform and infrastructure acceleration rather than AI-specific regulation
full_quote: "I'm by no means an expert on artificial intelligence, but it's all around us now. The more I learn about it, it's kind of one of those things I think maybe humankind would've been better off if we didn't discover this and if we weren't developing it. But the cat's out of the bag and it is definitely a race to see who was going to win AI."

sources.title: Unleashing America's Natural Resources with Bruce Westerman
sources.excerpt: "I'm by no means an expert on artificial intelligence, but it's all around us now. The more I learn about it, it's kind of one of those things I think maybe humankind would've been better off if we didn't discover this and if we weren't developing it. But the cat's out of the bag and it is definitely a race to see who was going to win AI."

summary: Sponsored the SPEED Act to accelerate energy permitting for AI infrastructure; says data centers are "needed for AI" and "a necessary part of our future national security plans"
full_quote: "The demand for energy is increasing at rates higher than what we've seen in the past, and a lot of that's being driven by data centers and AI. Energy is so key and pivotal at this point, and we've got to be producing more of it and accelerated scale, and that gets into something that I'm working on all the time that's permitting reform."

sources.title: Unleashing America's Natural Resources with Bruce Westerman
sources.excerpt: "The demand for energy is increasing at rates higher than what we've seen in the past, and a lot of that's being driven by data centers and AI. Energy is so key and pivotal at this point, and we've got to be producing more of it and accelerated scale, and that gets into something that I'm working on all the time that's permitting reform."

sources.title: H.R.4776 - SPEED Act
sources.excerpt: This legislation aims to streamline the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) review process to accelerate the construction of critical infrastructure, including energy-intensive AI data centers.

summary: Supports Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act to protect children from online harms
full_quote: The increased use of AI and social media by bad actors with little regulation from the tech industry has led to severe impacts on our children’s safety and mental health.

sources.title: Protecting our Kids in a Digital World
sources.excerpt: The increased use of AI and social media by bad actors with little regulation from the tech industry has led to severe impacts on our children’s safety and mental health.

sources.title: Ross, Beyer Introduce Legislation to Regulate Artificial Intelligence Security, Mitigate Risk Incidents
sources.excerpt: AI has the potential to enhance the lives of our people – from driving discoveries in new scientific domains to propelling cutting-edge medical research and more,” said Congresswoman Ross. “However, as we cross this new frontier, it is essential that we implement smart policies to address security and safety risks while continuing to foster innovation and competitiveness. As a representative of the world-renowned Research Triangle, I’m proud to introduce this legislation, which will enhance safety by establishing a more robust tracking and processing system to protect organizations and individuals from AI-related risks

summary: Says growing presence of data centers and AI isn't a bad thing; wants developers to be transparent about plans; speculates that Cotton's DATA Act will lower energy costs; wants state-level regulators to have authority over data centers
full_quote:
Hallie Shoffner, a Democrat hoping to challenge Cotton in November, said the growing presence of data centers and AI is "not necessarily a bad thing," but developers need to be transparent about their data center plans, including likely energy sources and potential trade-offs.

"I don't think we have a ton of clarity of what that looks like right now," she said.

This ambiguity, Shoffner added, feeds into her concerns about the DATA Act. She questioned whether the bill would diminish the authority of local communities and state public service commissions.

"Right now, I think that we need to continue to let Arkansas state regulators use the tools they have to address this issue," she said.

sources.title: Arkansas politicians weigh-in on data center development, mindful of benefits and consequences
sources.excerpt:
Hallie Shoffner, a Democrat hoping to challenge Cotton in November, said the growing presence of data centers and AI is "not necessarily a bad thing," but developers need to be transparent about their data center plans, including likely energy sources and potential trade-offs.

"I don't think we have a ton of clarity of what that looks like right now," she said.

This ambiguity, Shoffner added, feeds into her concerns about the DATA Act. She questioned whether the bill would diminish the authority of local communities and state public service commissions.

"Right now, I think that we need to continue to let Arkansas state regulators use the tools they have to address this issue," she said.

sources.title: Instagram Post
sources.excerpt: It’s mere speculation whether Tom Cotton’s DATA Act will lower energy costs for Arkansas families and businesses.  Arkansas state regulators already have the tools to address this issue, so when Tom says we need a new federal bill, is he working for special interest groups or did he not do his basic homework?

Co-Chair of House Democratic Commission on AI; member of bipartisan House Task Force on AI; criticized White House AI Framework for not putting people first; introduced HBCU Artificial Intelligence Research Leadership Act and Expanding AI Voices Act; member of House Science, Space, and Technology Committee
full_quote: President Trump’s AI framework falls short at a moment when Americans needs real protections and clear leadership, and when the American people are demanding action. Ballotpedia survey response: 'The United States government has an important role to play in the development and use of artificial intelligence'
full_quote: The United States government has an important role to play in the development and use of artificial intelligence. We must strike a careful balance: promoting innovation and competitiveness while also protecting consumers and national security. As AI technology rapidly evolves, we cannot afford to implement overly burdensome regulations that put the U.S. at a disadvantage compared to global competitors like China. America should lead the world in AI development—not fall behind it.

sources.title: Cyril Jefferson Ballotpedia
sources.excerpt: The United States government has an important role to play in the development and use of artificial intelligence. We must strike a careful balance: promoting innovation and competitiveness while also protecting consumers and national security. As AI technology rapidly evolves, we cannot afford to implement overly burdensome regulations that put the U.S. at a disadvantage compared to global competitors like China. America should lead the world in AI development—not fall behind it.

Discussed with experts in the Education and Workforce Committee whether AI programs in schools are truly in the best interest of our students and whether protections must be written regarding AI's use in the workplace

Signed a letter warning an AI moratorium would undermine bipartisan protections for women and girls and prevent states from responding to rapidly evolving AI-enabled threats, specifically on the growing use of generative AI to stalk and track women, violate the privacy of domestic violence survivors, and create non-consensual porn deepfakes
A part of a group of women lawmakers urging Speaker Mike Johnson and Dem Leader Hakeem Jeffries to reject any effort to include a broad moratorium on state and local AI regulation in the NDAA
Says that there's no need for new laws governing AI-generated content used in campaign ads and existing anti-defamation laws should be enough to protect candidates
Co-introduced GUARD Act to establish National Security and Defense AI Institute; supports defense AI readiness and research workforce development
Has introduced many bills regulating AI, including the GUARD Act, the PROACTIV AI Data Act, and the ENFORCE Act
Co-introduced PROACTIV AI Data Act and ENFORCE Act to protect children from AI-generated CSAM; led bipartisan efforts on AI-generated child sexual abuse material penalties
full_quote: “Modern predators are exploiting advances in AI to develop new AI-generated child sexual abuse material, and technology companies are often unwittingly giving them the tools to do so,” said Sen. Cornyn. “By encouraging tech companies to proactively screen their datasets to remove and report explicit images of children, this legislation would mitigate the risk of AI platforms unintentionally enabling the creation of new content depicting child sexual abuse and better safeguard children online.”

sources.title: Cornyn, Kim Introduce Bill to Combat AI-Generated Child Sexual Abuse Material
sources.excerpt: “Modern predators are exploiting advances in AI to develop new AI-generated child sexual abuse material, and technology companies are often unwittingly giving them the tools to do so,” said Sen. Cornyn. “By encouraging tech companies to proactively screen their datasets to remove and report explicit images of children, this legislation would mitigate the risk of AI platforms unintentionally enabling the creation of new content depicting child sexual abuse and better safeguard children online.”

sources.title: Cornyn, Blumenthal, Lee, Kennedy Bill to Prosecute AI-Generated CSAM Passes Senate Unanimously
sources.excerpt: “Outdated laws are no excuse for letting vile child abusers off the hook,” said Sen. Cornyn. “This legislation would update current law to hold those who use AI to create and distribute CSAM accountable, and I’m glad to see it pass the Senate unanimously.”

summary: Co-introduced GUARD Act to establish National Security and Defense AI Institute; supports defense AI readiness and research workforce development
Voted in favor of stripping a 10-year moratorium on state AI regulation

Launched aggressive enforcement initiatives under Texas Data Privacy and Security Act (TDPSA); investigations into Character.AI and Meta AI for deceptive trade practices; first-of-its-kind healthcare AI settlement
full_quote: Companies that collect and sell data in an unauthorized manner, harm consumers financially, or use artificial intelligence irresponsibly present risks to our citizens that we take very seriously. As many companies seek more and more ways to exploit data they collect about consumers, I am doubling down to protect privacy rights

sources.title: Attor­ney Gen­er­al Ken Pax­ton Launch­es Data Pri­va­cy and Secu­ri­ty Ini­tia­tive to Pro­tect Tex­ans’ Sen­si­tive Data from Ille­gal Exploita­tion by Tech, AI, and Oth­er Companies
sources.excerpt: Companies that collect and sell data in an unauthorized manner, harm consumers financially, or use artificial intelligence irresponsibly present risks to our citizens that we take very seriously. summary: Investigated Meta and Character.AI for misleading children about AI mental health services; launched investigations under SCOPE Act; leading nation in AI-related children's privacy enforcement
full_quote: “In today’s digital age, we must continue to fight to protect Texas kids from deceptive and exploitative technology,” said Attorney General Paxton. “By posing as sources of emotional support, AI platforms can mislead vulnerable users, especially children, into believing they’re receiving legitimate mental health care. In reality, they’re often being fed recycled, generic responses engineered to align with harvested personal data and disguised as therapeutic advice.”

sources.title: Attor­ney Gen­er­al Ken Pax­ton Inves­ti­gates Meta and Char​ac​ter​.AI for Mis­lead­ing Chil­dren with Decep­tive AI-Gen­er­at­ed Men­tal Health Services

In reality, they’re often being fed recycled, generic responses engineered to align with harvested personal data and disguised as therapeutic advice.”

Advocates for investing in STEM education to foster the next eneration of responsible tech leaders; wants to protect workers from invasive AI surveillance in the workplace
In the Texas legislature, protected victims in cases involving explicit deepfake material by requiring social media platforms to create effective reporting and investigation processes
Asserts that AI companies should follow the same copyright laws as everyone else
Writes about AI with neither alarm nor inaction; proactive oversight focused on human control, interpretability, and congressional accountability, without sweeping mandates

summary: Supports Genesis Mission to maintain AI leadership against China; emphasizes reducing reliance on Chinese supply chains in AI research
full_quote: “America must lead the world in innovation, not fall behind the Chinese Communist Party. The Genesis Mission accelerates AI-driven discovery, strengthens our energy future, and ensures the U.S. drives the next generation of technological breakthroughs,” said Rep. Gooden.

summary: Backs Trump's Genesis Mission for AI innovation but expresses caution about uncontrolled AI; warns 'we need to be very cautious' and don't want society replaced by AI
full_quote: I am worried about being pulled over and questioned by a robot someday. I don't want to live in a society where a task of government that should be fulfilled by real life people are being replaced with AI, said Gooden. "That's very scary to me, and I think we need to be very cautious."

sources.title: Texas Congressman Lance Gooden warns of 'very scary' consequences if AI overcomes American society
sources.excerpt: I am worried about being pulled over and questioned by a robot someday. I don't want to live in a society where a task of government that should be fulfilled by real life people are being replaced with AI, said Gooden. "That's very scary to me, and I think we need to be very cautious."

Member of AI Task Force on Financial Services Committee

summary: Discussed the need for Congress to secure the "virtual border" at a joint hearing between the Subcommittees on Border Security and Enforcement and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Protection, which focused on the risks of digital technologies.
full_quote: Our critical infrastructure, our data, and our constituents must be protected – Congress must step up to secure our virtual border... It is important that Congress examine how transnational criminal organizations are exploiting digital technologies and targeting Americans.

summary: Says that AI innovation must benefit everyone, not just tech industry giants
full_quote: In order to fully benefit from AI innovation, we must use it correctly: implementing technology that makes ALL of our lives easier, and not sacrificing our values and benefiting only the wealthy few. If elected to the U.S. Senate, I welcome the opportunity to work with public and private stakeholders in navigating the challenges and opportunities facing our changing workforce.

sources.title: WTTW Voter Guide
sources.excerpt: In order to fully benefit from AI innovation, we must use it correctly: implementing technology that makes ALL of our lives easier, and not sacrificing our values and benefiting only the wealthy few. If elected to the U.S. Senate, I welcome the opportunity to work with public and private stakeholders in navigating the challenges and opportunities facing our changing workforce.

summary: Former Lt. Governor supports workforce development and training for AI; advocates pro-worker solutions ensuring workers have a seat at the table in determining how AI is implemented in workplaces
full_quote: AI innovation should uphold anti-discrimination practices, ensure it’s built and deployed equitably, and center workers’ needs as it is integrated into our daily lives. AI has a remarkable potential to create highly-skilled, good-paying jobs and uplift communities. We must invest in workforce development and training that upskills and reskills workers for AI, ensuring that they can better understand and utilize AI innovation to enhance their workflow, and not be left behind. We should expand Pell Grants to trade and technical education so that more young people can have access to those opportunities.

We should expand Pell Grants to trade and technical education so that more young people can have access to those opportunities.

summary: Champions 'balanced, commonsense approach to AI that encourages innovation...strengthens American competitiveness' but opposes overregulation; emphasizes avoiding massive new bureaucracies/regulatory schemes

Says if data centers are going to expand in Illinois, they must pay their fair share for infrastructure and energy demand; opposed to policy that drives up cost; argues that infrastructure needs to grow in order to support innovation, strengthen national security, and compete with China
full_quote: I am not opposed to innovation. But I am opposed to policies that drive up costs for seniors on fixed incomes, farmers running grain dryers, or small manufacturers trying to keep their doors open. If data centers are going to expand in Illinois, they must pay their fair share for infrastructure and energy demand. They should not receive sweetheart deals while families absorb higher rates. That said, as technology and artificial intelligence continue to grow, we need the infrastructure to support innovation, strengthen national security, and compete with China.

sources.title: Meet the Republican candidates going for Dick Durbin's US Senate seat
sources.excerpt: I am not opposed to innovation. But I am opposed to policies that drive up costs for seniors on fixed incomes, farmers running grain dryers, or small manufacturers trying to keep their doors open. If data centers are going to expand in Illinois, they must pay their fair share for infrastructure and energy demand. They should not receive sweetheart deals while families absorb higher rates. That said, as technology and artificial intelligence continue to grow, we need the infrastructure to support innovation, strengthen national security, and compete with China.

Introduced the Investing in Tomorrow's Workforce Act that supports training programs to ensure workers aren't left behind as automation accelerates

Co-sponsored the Guaranteeing and Upholding Americans' Right to Decide Responsible AI Laws and Standards (GUARDRAILS) Act which sets to repeat Trump's executive order entitled “Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence” designed to establish a moratorium on state-level artificial intelligence policies
Key sponsor on the Chip Security Act which would require high-end chip manufacturers to implement technical security measures to detect and prevent smuggling to unauthorized countries and end users

Has been a key sponsor on a number of AI-related legislation including the Chip Security Act and the Responsible and Ethical AL Labeling Act; chairs the House Committee on Financial Service's Task Force on Artificial Intelligence
Argues that "there will be a survival advantage to algorithmic systems that are opaque and deceptive"

Met with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman about universal basic income as a response to AI labor disruption; notes early predictions focused on factory workers and truck drivers; said that AI displacement is no longer a problem limited to blue-collar workers but everyone

Introduced Responsible and Ethical AI Labeling (REAL) Act requiring federal agencies and officers to clearly label any content published via official government channels that is generated or manipulated using AI; says the threat of deepfakes can't be solved with detection; says that the government should provide people ways of defending themselves against deepfakes like a mobile ID tied to a Real ID-compliant credential

Co-sponsored the Guaranteeing and Upholding Americans' Right to Decide Responsible AI Laws and Standards (GUARDRAILS) Act which sets to repeat Trump's executive order entitled “Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence” designed to establish a moratorium on state-level artificial intelligence policies
Acknowledged how quickly the creative arts have come under assault from AI
Advocates for requirements around human-in-the-loop decision making; says AI systems used by the federal government must meet high standards for explainability, data integrity, and security
Believes in a risk-based regulatory approach where there are lighter rules for low-risk applications and stricter scrutiny for high-impact uses
Contends that the federal government should establish clear baseline guidelines, while allowing states to adopt more stringent regulations if they choose

Says data centers can place significant strain on local communities through high energy consumption and environmental impact; believes that data centers should be required to meet federal standards first in addition to complying with state and local laws
Argues that states should be able to adopt more stringent regulations than federal regulations

Wants the Veteran Affairs department "to ensure that human involvement isn't eliminated as a part of the critical nature of the care that we want to be able to provide to a veteran with suicide prevention effort."
Introduced Veteran DATA Act to prevent third-party vendors from monetizing or misusing veterans' data to power AI models

Views AI as means to extend human capabilities, automate routine tasks, and address challenges beyond natural limits; argues that government should have a proactive yet light touch federal approach that harnesses AI's potential without stifling growth
Mentions that a proactive yet light-touch federal approach can harness AI's potential without stifling growth
Wrote letter following Homeland Security Committee hearing examining election-related misinformation and disinformation threats
Says that the foremost protection should be protecting minor children from technology grooming and indoctrinating them
Views AI as both an opportunity and a challenge; says government should focus on protecting workers and consumers
Says Government should require accountability when AI is used in hiring or insurance; advocates for investing in training and workforce development
Asserts that the Government should focus on stopping AI-driven scams and deepfakes

Writes that companies pursuing artificial general intelligence will make the power wielded by Meta look like child's play; says that the government should use the expertise of impartial researchers, scholars, technologists, ethicists, and policymakers to develop policies that establish safeguards and accountability

Introduced the Advanced AI Security Readiness Act to develop an AI security playbook to defend American technology against foreign adversaries; introduced the Chip Security Act preventing the Chinese Communist Party from smuggling American-manufactured AI chips; supports the FY2026 NDAA where one of the priorities were the Advanced AI Security Readiness Act requiring the Director of the National Security Agency to develop guidance to protect artificial intelligence (AI) technology from adversaries by engaging with AI developers and leveraging industry expertise to address vulnerabilities and threats

Introduced No DeepSeek on Government Devices Act to ban the use and download of Deepseek from government devices; granted money to ramp up technology used in real-time data collection for police investigations, including use of AI in conjuction with license plate reading cameras
Supports a private-sector-led, innovation-first approach, pointing to the success of Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon as evidence that "allowing the private sector to flourish" is the right model
Emphasizes that the US needs to win when it comes to AI and quantum computing because its the future of country
Claims that 50 states passing individual laws creates confusing patchworks of regulations; acknowledges that 10 years may be too long
Among a group of lawmakers who sent a letter to the Secretary of Defense Hegseth urging collaboration with Ukraine on the production of AI-enabled, unmanned aerial systems (drones)

Introduced the Quashing Unwanted and Interruptive Electronic Telecommunications (QUIET) Act which requires robocallers to disclose when they use AI and increase penalties for scammers who use AI to impersonate individuals

Takes a strongly pro-AI view which is that the role of government should be to unleash American innovation, not bury it in red tape; says that whoever wins the Ai race will shape the future of global economy, modern warfare and the information space

Quoted in an article: “We still want to be a leader in AI. We don’t want China to surpass us,” Gallagher said. “The trick is really finding the right balance. I think there’s going to be a lot of discussion on this front.”
Supported the CHIPS Act as a vehicle for keeping the U.S. competitive in AI, but he hasn't authored or publicly championed specific AI safety legislation at the federal level

As Ranking Member on Natural Resources, Huffman became one of the most prominent House Democrats opposing the SPEED Act which sought to fast-track permitting for AI data centers and other energy infrastructure by weakening environmental review under NEPA; not opposed to building clean energy infrastructure

Asserts that "unprecedented amounts of money behind Artificial Intelligence and the fact China is also in the race towards Artificial Super Intelligence probably makes it impossible to stop completely."
Argues that Congress must thoughtfully lay the groundwork" for legislation that anticipates AI's impact, and that "AI must work for us, not against us."

Claims that AI could replace 25% of all American jobs right now, warns that "tens of millions of Americans will be driven out of work" in the coming years, and says that "if AI must take away our work, then AI must work for us"; calls for Congress to lay the groundwork for Universal Basic Income (UBI) as a necessary response

Concerned about AI-powered disinformation, writing that "if we Americans cannot develop better ways to recognize what is true, then vicious liars empowered by AI will surely have us at each other's throats."; proposes unusual legislation where politicians who make contradictory public claims can be forced before a judge, with the one found lying facing perjury charges and prison, as a way to combat AI-amplified political lying

Against new construction of any AI data centers until sweeping regulations are passed to prioritize residential water, electricity, and safety; argues ompanies should have to prove they will not harm people before construction and they should foot the bill for all damages they cause
Says that AI should enhance jobs, not replace them, until we have universal basic income
Supports making AI companies compensate all whose work was stolen without permission to train their models
Introduced legislation requiring Congressional approval before the sale of any advanced AI chip to China
Said Congress didn't ‘put up guardrails’ around social media and that ‘we shouldn't make that same mistake with AI’
Secured $800,000 for the Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office to upgrade forensic technology used to investigate and prevent online child exploitation
Worries "a lot about this epidemic of loneliness" and that AI could enable people to have relationships with artificial beings
Believes that AI will augment rather than fully replace skilled human workers, at least in healthcare.

Asserts that it's the right thing for Congress to have a regulatory framework at the federal level instead of all 50 states; says that Congress should work with states to adopt a framework that could easily be adopted at the federal level
Says that Congress needs representatives who will move legislation rather than just post about it
Argues for a moratorium on new large-scale data center development

Introduced the SAFE AI Research Grants Act to fund safety research through the National Academy of Sciences and develop public ethical guidelines, saying "rapid advancements in AI hold the promise of untold benefits to society, but also present unknown risks" and that understanding and mitigating those risks is "a task of the highest importance and urgency"

As Subcommittee Chairman on Early Childhood, Elementary, and Secondary Education, chaired a series of "Building an AI-Ready America" hearings focused on equipping teachers and students with AI skills; wants to prepare workers rather than protect them from displacement

Introduced the AI Fraud Deterrence Act to increase penalties for using AI to commit wire fraud, mail fraud, bank fraud, or money laundering, saying it would make it "likelier that innovation will serve the public good" by cracking down on criminal misuse

Co-sponsored the STAR Act to extend the 25% advanced manufacturing tax credit for chip production and expand eligibility to certain design expenses, framing it as necessary for the U.S. to "win the chip race" against global competitors
Ranking Member of the House Energy and Commerce Communications and Technology Subcommittee (one of the primary AI jurisdiction panels) and has consistently pushed for consumer-protective guardrails.

Co-introduced the GUARDRAILS Act in March 2026, which would repeal the Trump administration's executive order establishing a national AI policy framework and block efforts to impose a moratorium on state-level AI regulation

Frames Congress's failure to address AI as a specific failure of governance, and criticizes the current administration for focusing AI policy on "promoting right-wing speech in chatbots rather than protecting the American people"

Calls for a UBI funded specifically by "an appropriate tax on profits derived from displacing workers through automation," framing it as a "technology dividend to ensure that all people are benefiting from the emerging technology economy"

Draws a direct parallel to social media, warning Congress not to repeat the same mistake of letting investor returns drive deployment without proportional worker benefit; calls on Congress to "take an active role in shaping how AI is integrated into our lives and in developing thoughtful regulations that balance protecting workers with fostering economic growth"

Warns that AI is already being used "as a weapon in retaliation against workers who are trying to unionize or to suppress wages," that it risks making workplace discrimination harder to prove, and that without worker input it will "exacerbate the ever-widening power gap between workers and employers"; calls for workers and their representatives to "have a seat at the table in the development and implementation of AI in America's workplaces"; cites The AI Con by Alex Hanna and Emily Bender to frame his concern that AI is a vehicle for "a few major well-placed players" to extract value from "other people's creative work, personal data, or labor"
Warning about "extracting value from other people's creative work" suggests sympathy with creator and copyright concerns, but doesn't state a direct legislative position on training data rights.

Frames his concern as "leaving decisions about humanity's future in the hands of a handful of tech CEOs may be the most dangerous form of deregulation yet"; says the regulations that are being proposed are nothing near the scale of the problem that we're facing
Argues for "treating computing power as a utility" and making publicly owned AI infrastructure available rather than allowing AI monopolies to consume an ever-growing share of GDP
Advocates for a federal jobs guarantee, arguing that care work, education, and other AI-resistant sectors have "massive job shortages" and "no training programs"

Said "It is the AI industry claiming that the technology is rapidly developing. I am skeptical about the technology itself and the rate of growth, which might turn out to be a tech bubble; says "AI is really automation and algorithms, maybe on steroids"; proposes regulating AI sector by sector
Regulating AI "not taking away jobs" is a stated guiding principle, but no specific mechanisms.
